extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from a 4-rider breakaway after the peloton disintegrated in horrific weather, with THOMAS Geraint inheriting pink from COVID-withdrawn EVENEPOEL Remco.
tactical_patterns: VLASOV Aleksandr abandoned early while ill, showing GC contenders didn't wait for normal stage start to assess condition.
MILAN Jonathan contested intermediate sprint despite targeting stage win, burning matches that may have cost breakaway victory at 300m.
VINE Jay lost 10 minutes and top-10 GC by being dropped during clothing changes, showing service timing more critical than peloton position.
Four-rider breakaway survived because sprinter teams lost domestiques to attrition, leaving firepower insufficient despite closing to 1-minute at 15km.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: abandoned with COVID from race lead, exposing Quick-Step's lack of GC depth when leader withdraws
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: inherited pink by attrition rather than racing action, but positioned Ineos with four top-10 riders
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: abandoned hours into stage feeling ill, suggesting vulnerability to extreme weather conditions
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: lost 10 minutes and top-10 GC during service moment, indicating UAE support coordination issues
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: held power reserve until final 100m despite earlier attacks, showing superior tactical patience in 4-rider finale
rider_signals.GEE Derek: negative: attacked twice inside 1500m but lacked finishing speed, wasting energy that enabled CORT's win
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: regained contact after being distanced, demonstrating resilience despite reduced lead-out support
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: crashed during clothing change then never regained contact, showing vulnerability during in-race service
